item.title: Deals of the Week: One offshore wind project scores three big deals
item.description: Biggest offshore wind project in German Baltic Sea sealed record power deal with Amazon along with two other deals

item.title: Equinor quits another offshore wind market after abandoning renewables target
item.description: Norwegian oil giant recently scrapped its target for wind and solar deployment and raised its bar for investing in new renewables projects

item.title: 'Why not copy Iberdrola?': RWE grids play makes sense, say experts
item.description: Regulated grid assets now appear more profitable than building new renewables generation, which is a problem for the energy transition, said experts at roundtable hosted by Recharge News in association with Zurich Resilience Solutions

item.title: Wind power in demand as corporate giants face new rules
item.description: Night-time wind to attract premium as companies adjust to new demands to match green power generation closely to consumption, says procurement specialist

item.title: Orsted defeats huge damages claim concerning Danish power unit
item.description: Offshore wind champion may have avoided hundreds of millions of dollars in compensation payments

item.title: Investigation: How Israel controversy rocked a European wind giant
item.description: Eleven insiders speak out over events that rocked Denmark's Ramboll, including a deputy-head in wind division wearing Israel Defence Forces (IDF) clothing to company townhall, work for a controversial Israeli renewable energy developer and pushback against new payroll provider

item.title: 'Unacceptable': Wind giant condemns manager who wore IDF clothing to townhall event
item.description: Senior manager at Ramboll who wore Israel Defence Forces (IDF) clothing to virtual townhall caused uproar at one of the wind industry’s biggest names

item.title: Turkey reveals offshore tender plan with return option for developers
item.description: Energy ministry also sets support price ceiling and allows for negative bidding under certain conditions

item.title: Europe losing ground on energy storage to China and US, says Barclays chief
item.description: Europe and UK don't have that same tradition of helping businesses scale up as in US and China, Barclays' head of transition finance says

item.title: California fights Trump offshore wind buyout scheme threatening US project pipeline
item.description: Top state law enforcement and energy officials file notice of intent to sue over Ocean Winds’ Golden State deal, with Invenergy’s similar lease exit also in the frame

item.title: Germany to reshuffle offshore wind sites to tackle wake effects
item.description: Maritime agency BSH kicks off process to amend site development plan, with increased focus on actual power yields and economic viability of offshore wind projects

item.title: RWE buys majority in Amprion, turning regulated grids into third growth pillar
item.description: Utility to reach 55% stake in Amprion through $4.1bn transaction financed by large capital increase; renewables expansion remains on track

item.title: Hormuz crisis hits 'world's largest' onshore wind farm under construction
item.description: US-Iran conflict throttling global oil & gas shipments spills into clean energy supply chains, with Chinese wind turbine giant forced to divert shipment

item.title: Miliband and wind face uncertainty after UK's latest 'nervous breakdown'
item.description: Renewables-friendly energy secretary tipped as possible finance minister as Andy Burnham gets set to replace Keir Starmer – but leading analyst says offshore wind sector just needs 'boring consistency'

item.title: Utility buys stake in German offshore wind project after Amazon deal
item.description: Stadtwerke München to buy 25% in Gennaker offshore wind farm project that just sealed a PPA deal with internet retail titan Amazon

item.title: Enercon to outsource generator and tower production to embattled market outside EU
item.description: German premium wind turbine manufacturer says manufacturing partnership agreements will make supply chain more resilient

item.title: Chinese cables giant hires PwC to help choose European factory location
item.description: ZTT wants to build European factory to meet soaring demand for HVDC cables, but locals are investing too

item.title: Chinese cables giant weighing options on European factory location
item.description: ZTT wants to build European factory to meet soaring demand for HVDC cables, but locals are investing too

item.title: Demand for new German offshore wind auctions amid uncertainty over sites
item.description: French supermajor TotalEnergies has rocked German offshore wind sector by stating desire to hand back sites won in previous tenders, casting doubt on country’s pipeline

item.title: Largest renewable energy project 'in US history' comes online
item.description: The SunZia project, touted to deliver more power than the Hoover Dam, is now fully operational

item.title: Amazon strikes 'record power deal' for German offshore wind farm
item.description: Skyborn Renewables, a BlackRock subsidiary, says deal will give it confidence to build largest offshore wind farm planned in German Baltic Sea

item.title: Deals of the Week: Siemens Energy's big home win
item.description: Energy technology giant to build converter rig on the Baltic, while Chinese and Indian wind turbine makers Envision and Inox seal big orders in Asia

item.title: Canada wins dispute over stymied offshore wind project with US developer
item.description: New York-based renewable energy developer brought claim after being frustrated in efforts to build offshore wind farm on Lake Ontario

item.title: UK and Denmark join battle to meet data centre power demand
item.description: The two European nations have announced diverging measures as AI-driven electricity demand strains grids

item.title: The three specific renewables measures that can stop security threats – even the oil bosses are on board
item.description: Industrial leaders turn away from oil and gas and point to renewable energy to achieve energy security, according to new report

item.title: German energy minister unwilling to let TotalEnergies return offshore wind sites
item.description: Katherina Reiche reportedly insists that bids entered are legally binding contracts as oil firms seek to give back several gigawatts of sites won in 2023-25

item.title: Burnham vs Reform sets alarm bells ringing again for UK clean energy investors
item.description: Today's one-off election in Makerfield constituency in northwest England could signal a new direction for UK energy policy – more 'public control' or a Trump-style assault on clean power

item.title: Trump buys back more US offshore wind leases in continued war on sector
item.description: US President Donald Trump scuttles four more prospective offshore wind farms as continues working to redirect renewable energy investment into oil and gas

item.title: How Equinor has quietly raised its bar for offshore wind investment
item.description: Equinor appears to have tightened the test its offshore wind projects must pass before it is willing to commit capital

item.title: Siemens Energy wins massive deal as offshore converter rig production returns to Germany
item.description: German energy tech giant to build platform at Neptun wharf in Baltic Sea port together with Smulders as country moves to larger rigs in 2GW-plus category
